title: Mickelia oligarchica
intro: Mickelia oligarchica, also known as Schizolobium parahyba, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Americas, belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Mickelia oligarchica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mickelia oligarchica is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in the form of a tincture or resin, to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Mickelia oligarchica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. It belongs to the genus Mickelia and the order Alismatales. Further, it is categorized under the class Liliopsida and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification reflects its botanical identity and evolutionary relationships. Mickelia oligarchica is a member of the monocotyledonous plants, characterized by a single cotyledon during seed germination.
